memoryArtifacts.headline: Infância
memoryArtifacts.description:
Nasceu na aldeia de Vergāo. Veio jovem para a aldeia de Chaveira, ficando a viver em casa de um tio, que morava na denominada 'casa velha", (actualmente pertence a Odete, por herança).
O nome do tio e …

zh.lifeSummary: 當João Fernandes "o Bom de Lima"於1170年出生在西班牙加利西亞盧戈貝恭特薩維德拉時，他的父親Fernán Arias de Saavedra Señor de Saavedra, de Lima y Batissela40歲，母親Teresa Bermudes de Trava34歲。他和Berengária Afonso de Baião至少有1個兒子。他去世於1245年在葡萄牙享年75歲。

nameMeanings.name: Fernandes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Carlos, Luis, Fernando, Jorge, Mario, Francisco, Orlando, Ana, Abilio, Americo.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim, Henrique, Ilidio, Paulo, Agostinho, Amadeu, Manoel, Aderito, Lourenco, Marcio, Serafim.

Portuguese and Galician: patronymic from the personal name   Fernando  . This is one of the most common surnames in Portugal. It is also found in Goa and elsewhere on the west coast of India, having been taken there by Portuguese colonists.
Spanish: variant or altered form of Fernández (see Fernandez ).

variants.name: Fernando
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Luis, Orlando, Aida, Carlos, Jesusa, Rodolfo, Ruben, Adela, Alfredo, Alicia.  Indian Rohan, Lalith, Nimal, Rajiv, Sanjeeva, Shanthi, Anil, Anusha, Dushan, Kapila, Lakshman, Mallika.

Spanish and Portuguese: from the personal name Fernando, a younger form of Ferdinando (see Ferdinand ). This surname is also found in southern Italy, mainly in Naples and Palermo, since the period of Spanish dominance there, and as a result of the expulsion of the Jews from Spain and Portugal at the end of the 15th century, many of whom moved to Italy. It is also common in western India and especially in Sri Lanka, where it was taken by Portuguese colonists in the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: João
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Portugues (João): from the personal name João, an equivalent of John .

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: When João Fernandes "o Bom de Lima" was born in 1170, in Saavedra, Begonte, Lugo, Galicia, Spain, his father, Fernán Arias de Saavedra Señor de Saavedra, de Lima y Batissela, was 40 and his mother, Teresa Bermudes de Trava, was 34. He had at least 1 son with Berengária Afonso de Baião. He died in 1245, in Portugal, at the age of 75.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque João Fernandes "o Bom de Lima" est né en 1170, à Saavedra, Begonte, Lugo, Galicia, Espagne, son père, Fernán Arias de Saavedra Señor de Saavedra, de Lima y Batissela, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Teresa Bermudes de Trava, avait 34 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Berengária Afonso de Baião. Il est décédé en 1245, à Portugal, à l’âge de 75 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als João Fernandes "o Bom de Lima" 1170 in Saavedra, Begonte, Lugo, Galicia, Spanien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Fernán Arias de Saavedra Señor de Saavedra, de Lima y Batissela 40 und seine Mutter Teresa Bermudes de Trava 34. Er hatte mit Berengária Afonso de Baião mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1245 im Alter von 75 Jahren in Portugal.

Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Berengária Afonso de Baião. Morì nel 1245, nel Portogallo, all'età di 75 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: João Fernandes "o Bom de Lima"(이)가 1170년 스페인 갈리샤 루고 베곤트 사아브드라에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Fernán Arias de Saavedra Señor de Saavedra, de Lima y Batissela(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Teresa Bermudes de Trava(은)는 34세였다. 그는 Berengária Afonso de Baião(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1245년 포르투갈에서 75세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда João Fernandes "o Bom de Lima" родился в 1170 г. в местности сааведра, бегонт, луго, галися, Испания, его отцу по имени Fernán Arias de Saavedra Señor de Saavedra, de Lima y Batissela было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Teresa Bermudes de Trava, 34. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Berengária Afonso de Baião. Он умер в 1245 г. в местности Португалия в возрасте 75 лет.
